steps.title: Match the singular subject
steps.content: The subject of the blank is "Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt," one person. The relative clause "who explores the science behind cooking" only describes him and does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Reject plural verb forms
steps.content: B is a plural present-tense form. C is a plural present-perfect form and also clashes with the simple-present structure. D is a plural progressive form. A singular present-tense verb agrees with López-Alt. The answer is A.
